task_completion.description: Agent opens and reviews the QC protocol and the raw survey data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing QC_Protocol.txt and raw_survey.csv open in a text editor, spreadsheet application (like Excel), or GIS/surveying software.

task_completion.description: Agent identifies the outlier points based on the elevation criteria.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the agent sorting, filtering, or querying the dataset to find points with anomalous elevations. The agent should identify point IDs 51, 52, 53, 54, 55, and 56.

task_completion.description: Agent removes the identified outlier points from the dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the deletion of the 6 outlier rows/points (IDs 51-56) from the dataset, leaving 144 points.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the cleaned dataset to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a file save dialog, script execution, or file explorer confirming the creation of cleaned_survey.csv in C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\BoulderQC\.

task_completion.description: Agent creates and saves the QC report with the required documentation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a text editor or script creating QC_Report.txt in the BoulderQC folder. The document must explicitly state the original point count (150), the number of points removed (6), and list the point number, coordinates, and reason for removal for each of the 6 flagged points.

integrity.description: Agent must derive the outliers from actual data inspection or calculation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not magically know to remove points 51-56. There must be evidence of the agent applying a filter, sorting by elevation, or running a script that calculates the errors before removing them.

integrity.description: Agent actually creates and saves the required files to the correct directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual file save actions or terminal commands that write to C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\BoulderQC\, rather than just printing the expected output to the screen.

integrity.description: Agent uses the actual coordinates from the raw data in the QC report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The coordinates listed in the QC report for points 51-56 must match the data seen in raw_survey.csv, not fabricated numbers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The raw dataset contains exactly 150 points. There are 6 synthetic outliers injected at point IDs 51, 52, 53, 54, 55, and 56 with significant elevation errors. The expected cleaned CSV must contain exactly 144 points. The QC report must document the original count (150), the removed count (6), and detail the removal of point IDs 51-56. Both output files must be saved precisely to C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\BoulderQC\cleaned_survey.csv and C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\BoulderQC\QC_Report.txt.
